heroHeadline: FOMC Holds 3.50–3.75% at Powell's Final Meeting — Warsh Clears Senate Banking 13-11 Party-Line; Brent Crude Surges to $116 on New Trump-Iran Threats
heroSubtitle: Apr 29: The FOMC voted unanimously to hold the federal funds rate at 3.50–3.75% at what is almost certainly Jerome Powell's final scheduled meeting as Fed Chair (term expires May 15). Simultaneously, the Senate Banking Committee voted 13-11 on an unprecedented party-line basis to advance Kevin Warsh's nomination — the first such partisan committee vote for a Fed Chair in Senate history. Brent crude surged 4.74% to $114–116/bbl (highest since June 2022) as Trump issued new Iran threats, deepening the stagflation trap the day before the critical Q1 2026 GDP advance estimate (BEA, April 30; Atlanta GDPNow: 1.24%) and March PCE inflation data. BOJ held at 0.75% on April 28 with the most hawkish internal split of the Ueda era (6-3 vote; 3 dissenters pushed for 1.00%), cutting its FY2026 GDP forecast to 0.5% and raising its core inflation forecast to 2.8%. Conference Board Consumer Confidence rose unexpectedly to 92.8 — highest of 2026 — but the Expectations Index (72.2) remains below the 80 recession-warning threshold for the 15th consecutive month. S&P 500 fell to ~7,128 as mega-cap tech (MSFT, META, GOOGL, AMZN — ~$11T market cap) reported Q1 earnings after close. Recession probabilities: Goldman Sachs 30%, JPMorgan 35%, Moody's Analytics 49%.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, US Census Bureau, S&P Global Flash PMI Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of Japan, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, NPR, CBS News, NBC News, TheStreet, Yahoo Finance, Fortune, Washington Post, Morningstar, Senate Banking Committee, Japan Times, Polymarket, Nomura, Benzinga.
